name: Kuromaku
short_description: Kuromaku | 黒幕 is an indie first-person psychological thriller experienced through the perspective of a child left alone for three days. Complete everyday routines, uncover secrets, and endure unsettling encounters as your choices shape how the story unfolds Ahead.
detailed_description:
KUROMAKU

A VHS-style psychological thriller inspired by Fears to Fathom and Chilla’s Art. Ordinary routines become your only defense against a creeping, unseen horror in this slow-burn 1990s-inspired nightmare.

Kuromaku is a narrative-focused first-person horror experience built around isolation, domestic realism, and psychological tension. Rather than relying on constant spectacle, the game draws fear from silence, subtle environmental shifts, and the growing feeling that something inside the house is watching.

About the Game

Kuromaku is a first-person psychological horror experience centered around immersion, exploration, and player-driven outcomes.

Dual Visual Modes

  • Normal Mode – Clean, immersive presentation.

  • VHS Mode – Optional retro analog filter inspired by 1990s camcorder footage, featuring grain, distortion, and signal interference.

    Everyday Routines, Twisted

    Progress time by completing grounded household tasks:

    • Cleaning the bathroom

    • Washing dishes

    • Buying groceries

    • Maintaining daily responsibilities

    The more routine life becomes, the more unsettling the changes feel.

    Exploration & Environmental Storytelling

    Search the house and surrounding areas. Examine objects. Pay attention to subtle details. The environment evolves over time, revealing clues and hidden implications. Lighting, ambient sound, and environmental tension shift across the three days, transforming safe domestic spaces into oppressive environments.

    Multiple Endings

    Different choices lead to different conclusions.
    Not every truth is meant to be uncovered. Your reactions determine how events unfold.
    Confront the presence. Ignore it. Investigate further.

    Every decision influences the final outcome.

reviews.review_text: I did not play the Demo to this game and I am actually so happy that I didnt! What an amazing 1st game this is and the Devs were so kind! The scares in this were perfectly timed and they added in some super creepy elements that horror lovers and tru crime junkies will absolutely LOVE!!! Thank you so much for this experience! W game! W devs!

played kuromaku and i enjoyed the story and the scares that came with it for being their first game i think they did pretty well only issue for me was i would have liked the dialogue to be a bit faster as i am a faster reader lol

Played this game on stream, baught it on release day and had  no idea what to expect but it looked fun.
I had a fun time playing it and will try and will play again to try get all the endings.
great job for your first game
